reference_population: Active head coaches with 34+ games (2024 season)

ats_win_pct_z.what: Percentage of games where the team beat the closing spread.
ats_win_pct_z.how: (games where point differential exceeded the closing spread) / (total games with a spread on record).
ats_win_pct_z.limits: Spread data only available from 2006 on. Older coaches have shorter ATS histories.

fourth_down_epa_z.what: Average Expected Points Added on 4th-down plays.
fourth_down_epa_z.how: Mean of nflverse EPA on all 4th-down plays (pass, run, punt, FG) where this coach was on offense.
fourth_down_epa_z.limits: Does not directly compare to the analytically correct decision. Bold coaches who go for it and convert look great; conservative coaches who always punt get league-average results.

close_game_win_pct_z.what: Win percentage in games decided by 7 points or fewer.
close_game_win_pct_z.how: (wins in games with absolute point diff <= 7) / (total such games).
close_game_win_pct_z.limits: Close-game results are famously noisy. Even good coaches go .500 in close games over small samples.

record_vs_winning_win_pct_z.what: Win percentage against opponents that finished 9 or more wins that season.
record_vs_winning_win_pct_z.how: (wins vs. winning teams) / (games vs. winning teams).
record_vs_winning_win_pct_z.limits: Fraud check, but sample sizes are smaller than full-season record. 9-win cutoff is a judgement call.

pre_snap_penalty_rate_z.what: Pre-snap penalties per play while this coach was coaching (both sides of the ball).
pre_snap_penalty_rate_z.how: (false starts, delay of game, illegal formation/shift/motion, encroachment, 12-men, etc.) / (total plays).
pre_snap_penalty_rate_z.limits: Measures team discipline, which is attributable to coaching but also to roster composition. Rookie-heavy rosters commit more pre-snap penalties regardless of coach.

rz_td_rate_z.what: Percentage of red zone offensive plays that ended in a touchdown.
rz_td_rate_z.how: (red zone pass TDs + rush TDs) / (red zone pass + rush plays).
rz_td_rate_z.limits: True red-zone efficiency should be measured per trip, not per play. This play-rate proxy rewards teams that score fast in the red zone and penalizes those that need multiple plays.

post_bye_win_pct_z.what: Win percentage in the first game after a bye week.
post_bye_win_pct_z.how: (wins in post-bye games) / (post-bye games). Post-bye = gap greater than 1 week in the schedule.
post_bye_win_pct_z.limits: Tiny sample per coach - usually 1 post-bye game per season. Very noisy.

timeout_efficiency_z.what: Average timeouts remaining entering the 2-minute warning of each half.
timeout_efficiency_z.how: Mean of timeouts remaining at the first play with 120 or fewer seconds left in Q2 and Q4, averaged across all games.
timeout_efficiency_z.limits: Higher is not always better - a coach SHOULD sometimes spend timeouts in the 2nd or 4th quarter. This rewards hoarding, which is not always optimal.
